change_reason: Record no-bridge-bypass protected behavior from DELIB-S347
explicit_change_request: AUQ DELIB-S347-PROJECT-SCOPED-IMPLEMENTATION-AUTHORIZATION: Owner rejected skipping per-proposal bridge review or implementation-start controls under project authorization.
full_content:
## Protected Behavior

Project authorization must not bypass the Prime Builder / Loyal Opposition bridge lifecycle. A valid project authorization can satisfy the owner-approval portion of bounded project work, but implementation still requires proposal review, latest bridge GO, implementation-start target-path authorization, spec-derived tests, implementation report, and Loyal Opposition verification.

## Required Behavior

- Implementation-start authorization must still require latest bridge GO and approved proposal target paths.
- A project authorization must not broaden target paths beyond the approved proposal.
- A project authorization must be current, active, unexpired, and tied to the cited project.
- If a proposal cites a work item, that work item must be an active member of the authorized project or explicitly included by the authorization record.

## Evidence

Owner decision: `DELIB-S347-PROJECT-SCOPED-IMPLEMENTATION-AUTHORIZATION`.
Bridge approval: `bridge/gtkb-project-scoped-implementation-authorization-004.md`.
